label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Since February usually has 28 days, except in leap years when it has 29. But 2001 is not a leap year, right? Because leap years are every 4 years, like 2000, 2004, etc. So 2001 is not a leap year. So the last day of February 2001 would be February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So that means today's date is February 28, 2017, because 2001 plus 16 years is 2017. Wait, is that right? Let me check. 2001 to 2017 is 16 years. So yes, if she was born in 2001, then 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today is February 28, 2017. The question is asking what the date was a month ago from today. So we need to subtract one month from February 28, 2017. If today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017, right? Because going back one month from February is January, and the day is still 28. But wait, let me think again. What if the month doesn't have 28 days? Well, January does have 31 days, so January 28 exists. So a month before February 28 is January 28. So the date would be 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017.
